summary: In an interview ahead of the 2024 Alzheimer's Association International Conference, Dr. Virginia M.-Y. Lee emphasizes that the future of Alzheimer's disease and related dementias (ADRD) research must focus on the tau protein. She argues that while current clinical trials heavily target beta-amyloid plaques, tau is the true driver of neurodegeneration, and developing therapies to modulate tau is critical for effective treatment.

principles.statement: Tau is the primary driver of neurodegeneration in Alzheimer's disease and related dementias, not beta-amyloid.
principles.rationale: While current treatments focus on clearing amyloid plaques, tau neurofibrillary tangles are the actual cause of severe damage and inflammation in the brain.
principles.source_quote: It is generally agreed upon that tau is really the killer in ADRDs, not beta amyloid.

principles.statement: Future drug development for ADRDs must focus on understanding and modulating tau.
principles.rationale: Currently, clinical trials lack treatments that reduce tau tangles. Understanding tau regulation will allow for the development of therapies that can upregulate or downregulate it as needed.
principles.source_quote: If we can understand what regulates and modulates tau, then we will be able to develop medication and therapies that could either upregulate or downregulate tau.

signature_quotes.text: It is generally agreed upon that tau is really the killer in ADRDs, not beta amyloid.
signature_quotes.context: Discussing the need to shift focus from beta-amyloid therapeutics to tau-based treatments.

signature_quotes.text: The current treatment is to get rid of amyloid plaques. However, there is really nothing right now in clinical trials that reduce tau neurofibrillary tangles.
signature_quotes.context: Highlighting the gap in current Alzheimer's clinical trials.

signature_quotes.text: If we can understand what regulates and modulates tau, then we will be able to develop medication and therapies that could either upregulate or downregulate tau.
signature_quotes.context: Explaining the future direction of ADRD drug development.

signature_quotes.text: It is really about time. I am glad that I’ve worked for so long because I am able to see what is happening, and it makes me very happy.
signature_quotes.context: Reflecting on the increasing number of women presenting at the Alzheimer's Association International Conference.

anti_patterns.description: Exclusively targeting beta-amyloid plaques in Alzheimer's clinical trials while ignoring tau.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: Beta-amyloid is only one part of the pathology. Tau neurofibrillary tangles are the actual 'killer' causing damage in ADRDs, and failing to target them leaves the primary driver of the disease unchecked.
